company: Pike County Light & Power Company / Leatherstocking Gas Company LLC
company_raw: Pike County Light & Power Company / Leatherstocking Gas Company LLC
source_note: Management & Operations Audit of Pike County Light & Power Company and Leatherstocking Gas Company LLC, PA PUC Bureau of Audits; PUC publication 2025-07-10, Docket No. D-2024-3050338. PDF: puc.pa.gov/pcdocs/1886204.pdf. Findings & recommendations parsed verbatim from the Exhibit I-2 Summary of Recommendations.

findings.title: Executive Management, Organizational Structure, and Safety

recommendations.text: Expand the succession planning process to include short-term contingency plans for the PCLP General Manager, and all key employees.
Formally document procedures for all human resources filing and reporting activities to ensure compliance.
Implement a system to govern oversight of the review, update and documentation of all policies, and create a policy for centralized management of the system.
Expand staffing at PCLP to ensure optimal electric operations.
Establish an overarching internal control policy.
File an updated AIA which accurately reflects the intercompany transactions occurring between PCLP, LGC, and their affiliates.
Conduct routine periodic testing of intercompany transactions to ensure shared costs are distributed in accordance with approved methodologies.
Compare total actual costs for internally performed shared services and the cost of outside services to ensure cost effectiveness for all entities regularly.
Establish internal controls over intercompany transactions and cost allocations processes and comply with all aspects of the approved AIA.
Establish a process to routinely review and update financial management policies and procedures and ensure version control is in place.
Document an internal dividend policy that provides advance notice and written explanation to the Commission for each dividend payment PCLP and LGC make in excess of 85% of net income.
Document cash management procedures and establish guidelines for assessment of cash positions.
Develop and document guidelines and policies for budget creation and management including the regular reporting of significant budget variance explanations.
Develop and maintain a comprehensive damage prevention manual that details appropriate excavating procedures for PCLP’s electric utility infrastructure and assets.
Update PCLP’s storm response plan after a storm-related event or at least annually.
Establish and maintain a collection system for excavation damages and increase public awareness efforts for all involved parties about their responsibilities under PA One Call.
Review the UFG calculation, including the adjustment factor, and document all steps to ensure accuracy of reporting.
Correct minor physical security deficiencies at PCLP and LGC and perform periodic physical security reviews of all facilities.
Develop comprehensive business continuity plans for PCLP and LGC and review, test, and update them annually.
Develop and maintain physical security plans for PCLP and LGC and review, update, and test annually.
Annually review, test, and update emergency response plans for PCLP and LGC, and update PCLP’s emergency response plan to detail company response to non-storm related emergencies.
Formally document customer service policies and procedures to govern customer service practices.
Revise the tariff and other customer-facing documentation to accurately reflect PCLP’s and LGC’s budget billing processes.
Explore eliminating manual meter reading at LGC to streamline operations, reduce errors, and efficiently use staff.
Improve the customer experience by expanding payment options and eliminating the manual process for budget billing.
